principles.name: Internal Governance Structures and Measures
principles.definition: Organisations using AI in decision-making should ensure that appropriate internal governance structures and measures are in place.
principles.description: Establish clear roles and responsibilities for AI system oversight. Implement risk management and internal controls proportionate to the nature and extent of AI use.
keyElements: Clear roles and responsibilities for ethical AI use
Risk management processes for AI deployment
Regular review and update of governance measures

principles.name: Determining AI Decision-Making Model
principles.definition: Organisations should determine the level of human involvement in AI-augmented decision-making.
principles.description: Three levels: human-in-the-loop (human makes decision with AI input), human-over-the-loop (human monitors AI decisions with override ability), and human-out-of-the-loop (fully automated). Level should match risk profile.
keyElements: Risk-based determination of human involvement level

principles.name: Operations Management
principles.definition: Organisations should implement robust operations management practices for AI systems.
principles.description: Covers data management, model development and deployment, performance monitoring, and system maintenance throughout the AI lifecycle.

principles.name: Stakeholder Interaction and Communication
principles.definition: Organisations should interact with and communicate to stakeholders in an open and transparent manner.
principles.description: Proactive communication about AI use, providing accessible information to affected individuals, and establishing feedback and redress mechanisms.
keyElements: Proactive disclosure of AI use to stakeholders
Redress channels for adverse AI decisions

agenticAi.title: Agentic AI Addendum
agenticAi.description: Supplementary guidance addressing emerging risks from agentic AI systems — autonomous AI agents that can plan, use tools, and take actions with minimal human oversight.
keyConsiderations: Enhanced oversight for autonomous AI agents
Clear boundaries and guardrails for AI agent actions
Logging and auditability of AI agent decision chains
Risk assessment for multi-step autonomous workflows
Liability and accountability for AI agent actions
